call. When Claude decides to use a tool, it returns a

content.text: Unlike OpenAI, Anthropic returns tool arguments as a Python
required. Tool results go back as a
Every Claude tool has three fields:
After executing the tool, you return results in a
links each result to the originating call:

import json

# Execute the tool
tool_block = response.content[1]
result_str = json.dumps({"temp_c": 22, "condition": "clear", "humidity": 65})

# Build the tool_result message
tool_result_message = {
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
        {
            "type": "tool_result",
            "tool_use_id": tool_block.id,   # must match exactly
            "content": result_str,           # must be a string
        }
    ],
}

# Continue the conversation
messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in Tokyo?"},
    {"role": "assistant", "content": response.content},   # entire content list
    tool_result_message,
]

final = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=[weather_tool],
    messages=messages,
)
print(final.stop_reason)   # end_turn
print(final.content[0].text)
# "The weather in Tokyo is currently 22°C with clear skies and 65% humidity."
A complete, error-handling agent loop for Anthropic tool use:
Claude often produces a short text preamble before a tool call — for example, "Let me check the weather for you." The response
blocks. Iterate over all blocks and filter by type:
block. Claude reads this flag and adjusts its response — typically retrying with different arguments or explaining the failure to the user:
Every tool call and its result must stay in the conversation history. Pass the full
list to each subsequent API call — Claude needs the prior context to reason about the results correctly.
The streaming API delivers tool call arguments incrementally as
to obtain the complete tool calls without manually accumulating deltas.

Claude generally produces well-formed tool arguments, but untrusted or complex schemas warrant a validation step before execution. Pydantic makes this a one-liner and returns clean error details if the model hallucinated a field.
Parallel Tool Calls in a Single Response
When Claude can infer that multiple tool calls are independent, it may issue several
